data.title: Gold slips below ₹1.55 lakh per 10 grams in India, silver plunges over 3%
data.description:
Gold and silver prices declined sharply in Indian markets on Monday (February 16), tracking weak global cues and profit-booking after last week’s rally driven by softer US inflation data.

On the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX), gold futures for April 2026 delivery fell nearly 1%, slipping below the ₹1.55 lakh per 10 grams mark. Silver futures for March 2026 delivery dropped over 3%, trading below ₹2.37 lakh per kg.

The domestic decline followed losses in international markets. In early Asian trade, gold fell 1.1% to $4,994.60 per ounce, dropping back below the $5,000 an ounce psychological level. Silver declined 3.8% to $75.04 per ounce.

Analysts attributed the correction to profit-booking and thin global trade, with US markets closed for the Presidents Day holiday and Chinese markets shut for the Lunar New Year.

Last week, bullion had rallied after softer-than-expected US inflation strengthened expectations of monetary easing by the Federal Reserve. US consumer inflation rose 2.4% year-on-year in January, lower than market estimates and down from December’s 2.7%.

The data pulled down US Treasury yields and increased market bets on rate cuts later this year.

Manav Modi, Commodities Analyst at Motilal Oswal Financial Services, said that markets are currently pricing in the possibility of multiple rate cuts this year, which could support bullion inflows.

Despite Monday’s fall, analysts said geopolitical tensions and safe-haven demand continue to underpin sentiment. Reports of the US deploying the USS Gerald R Ford to the Middle East amid stalled Iran nuclear talks have kept risk perceptions elevated.

Market participants are now focusing on upcoming US macroeconomic indicators, particularly the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index and minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC), for further clarity on the interest rate trajectory.

According to Axis Securities’ weekly commodity derivatives snapshot, both metals have witnessed heightened volatility amid cross-asset movements and shifting rate expectations.

Analysts said that in the near term, gold and silver prices in India are likely to remain sensitive to global price action, US data releases and movements in bond yields and the dollar, with volatility expected to persist.

-With Reuters inputs

data.title: India's wholesale prices rise 1.81% y/y in January
data.description:
India's wholesale prices (INWPI=ECI) rose 1.81% year-on-year in January after a 0.83% rise a month ago, government data showed on Monday.

Economists polled by Reuters had projected the wholesale prices to rise 1.25% year-on-year in January.

The rise in inflation in January is primarily due to the higher cost of manufacturing basic metals, prices of food and textiles, among others, the data showed.

KEY NUMBERS

• Wholesale food prices rose 1.41% year-on-year in January after being flat a month ago.

• Vegetable prices rose 6.78% year-on-year in January after declining 3.5% year-on-year a month ago.

• Prices of manufactured products increased 2.86% year-on-year in January as compared to a 1.82% rise in December.

• Fuel and power prices dropped 4.01% year-on-year in January against a 2.31% fall in December.

data.title: Pharol Says Court Recognises Claim For Over 750 Mln Euros Regarding Rioforte Insolvency
data.description:
Pharol SGPS SA :

  • SAID ON FRIDAY, LUXEMBOURG COMMERCIAL COURT RECOGNISED PHAROL'S CLAIM ON INSOLVENCY ESTATE OF RIOFORTE, S.A. IN AMOUNT OF CAPITAL OF 750 MILLION EUROS

  • AMOUNT TO BE INCREASED BY INTEREST DUE UNTIL DATE OF OPENING OF INSOLVENCY PROCEEDINGS

  • COURT ALSO DECIDED THAT PHAROL DOES NOT HAVE TO RETURN ANY AMOUNTS TO ESPIRITO SANTO INTERNACIONAL (ESI) FOR AN ALLEGEDLY UNDUE PAYMENT MADE BY ESI IN 2014

  • COURT DECISIONS DO NOT IMPLY ANY TYPE OF ACCOUNTING TREATMENT IN PHAROL'S ACCOUNTS, NOR IMPACT 2025 RESULTS

data.title: Roche Says Its Blood Cancer Drug Shows Positive Results in Trial
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Roche said its blood cancer drug Gazyva/Gazyvaro demonstrated positive results in patients with primary membranous nephropathy, a rare, autoimmune kidney disease.

The Swiss drugmaker on Monday said that results from a late-stage trial showed statistically significant and clinically meaningful benefits, reflecting that more patients achieved complete remission at two years.

The drug is already approved in the U.S. and the European Union for the treatment of adults with active lupus nephritis, as well as in 100 countries for various types of haematological cancers, Roche said.

The company will present results at an upcoming medical meeting, it added.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: Finnish-German Bond Yield Spread Expected to Tighten — Market Talk
data.description: The 10-year Finnish-German government bond yield spread has widened modestly ahead of Tuesday's auction, but is expected to tighten again, Danske Bank's Kirstine Kundby-Nielsen says in a note. This prospect should attract investors to Tuesday's Finnish bond auction, recommending investors "to buy some cheap Finnish government bonds." The Finnish State Treasury will auction 1.5 billion euros in September 2035- and April 2041-dated government bonds on Tuesday. The 10-year Finnish-German yield spread closed at 27 basis points on Friday, according to LSEG data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Middle East Crude-Dubai rises for fourth session
data.description:
Middle East crude benchmark Dubai inched up for a fourth session on Monday, with uncertainty in Iranian supply and tensions in the region supporting the market.

U.S. and Iranian officials are due to hold a second round of talks in Geneva on Tuesday after renewing negotiations earlier this month aimed at tackling their decades-long dispute over Tehran's nuclear programme and averting a new military confrontation.

Singapore oil markets will be closed on Tuesday and Wednesday for Lunar New Year holidays.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

Cash Dubai's premium to swaps inched up 5 cents to $1.04 a barrel, climbing for a fourth session.

PetroChina will deliver an April-loading Upper Zakum crude cargo to Mercuria while ExxonMobil will deliver an al-Shaheen cargo to TotalEnergies following the deals.

NEWS

OPEC+ is leaning towards a resumption in oil output increases from April, three OPEC+ sources said, as the group prepares for peak summer demand and price strength is bolstered by tensions over U.S.-Iran relations.

The U.S. eased sanctions on Venezuela's energy sector on Friday, issuing two general licenses that allow global energy companies to operate oil and gas projects in the OPEC member and for other companies to negotiate contracts to bring in fresh investments.

Valero Energy is set to buy up to 6.5 million barrels of Venezuelan crude in March bound for its Gulf Coast refineries, making it the top foreign refiner of the OPEC country's oil since the United States captured President Nicolas Maduro in January, sources said on Friday.

Marine fuel sales at the world's largest bunker hub of Singapore posted a strong start to 2026, driven by healthy demand and higher price premiums, based on port data and trade sources.
